assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan explicitly lists all four files under 'Files Inspected / Analyzed Before Planning': get.rs (#1), advisory.rs (#2), summary.rs (#3), and error.rs (#6), with descriptions of what was studied in each.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan lists exactly the 3 files to create (severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint, advisory_summary.rs test) and 3 files to modify (model/mod.rs, endpoints/mod.rs, service/advisory.rs). No unrelated files are listed for modification or creation.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's 'Files to Create' section lists: (1) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, (2) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, (3) tests/api/advisory_summary.rs — all three required files.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's 'Files to Modify' section lists: (4) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs, (5) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, (6) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs — all three required files.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: The commit message is 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation service and endpoint' which follows the type(scope): description format. The footer contains 'Refs: TC-9201' which references the task ID.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: The 'Git Command' section explicitly shows: git commit -m "..." --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code'

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: The plan states 'Working Branch: TC-9201 (branched from main)' and shows the git command 'git checkout -b TC-9201 main'. The Target Branch is identified as 'main'.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: The conventions.md explicitly identifies both patterns: (1) 'Module Structure Pattern' describing the 'model / service / endpoints tri-layer architecture', and (2) 'Error Handling' pattern noting 'Result<T, AppError>' and '.context("description of what failed")'.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: The plan explicitly states 'Target Branch: main' near the top of the document, and the git command branches from main: 'git checkout -b TC-9201 main'.
